content.problemStatement:
You are given a graph with NNN nodes and MMM edges. Each edge has a value associated with it. There are no self-loops and multiple edges in the graph.

Each edge connects two nodes. The cost of going from the first node of the edge to the second via the edge is equal to the value of the edge whereas the cost of going in the reverse direction is equal to the negative of it.

Your task is to figure out if the graph is conservative or not. A graph is conservative if and only if for each pair of nodes, the cost of reaching the one node from the other is the same via all possible paths. The cost of a path is simply the sum of the costs of each edge in the path.

###Input:


First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains M+1M+1M+1 lines of input.
The first line contains the integers NNN and MMM.
The next MMM lines contain the description of the edges. Each line contains the integers uuu vvv www denoting that an edge exists between the nodes uuu and vvv with the value www.
It is guaranteed that if there is an edge between uuu to vvv then there will be no edge from vvv to uuu.
###Output:
For each testcase, output "YES" if the graph is conservative otherwise "NO"
###Constraints


1‚â§T‚â§1051 \leq T \leq 10^51‚â§T‚â§105
1‚â§N‚â§1051 \leq N \leq 10^51‚â§N‚â§105
1‚â§M‚â§min(105,(N‚àó(N‚àí1))/2)1 \leq M \leq min(10^5,(N*(N-1))/2)1‚â§M‚â§min(105,(N‚àó(N‚àí1))/2)
1‚â§u,v‚â§N1 \leq u,v \leq N1‚â§u,v‚â§N
‚àí109‚â§w‚â§109-10^9 \leq w \leq 10^9‚àí109‚â§w‚â§109
The sum of NNN over all testcases is less than equal to 10610^6106.
The sum of MMM over all testcases is less than or equal to 10610^6106.
###Sample Input:
2
4 4
1 2 2
2 3 1
4 1 -1
3 4 -2
4 4
1 2 2
2 3 2
1 4 2
4 3 1

###Sample Output:
YES
NO

###EXPLANATION:
In the second testcase, the path 1‚àí>2‚àí>31->2->31‚àí>2‚àí>3 has cost 444 while the path 1‚àí>4‚àí>31->4->31‚àí>4‚àí>3 has cost 333
